mission.title: traefik: Let's Encrypt: use ACME API V2
mission.description: Let's Encrypt: use ACME API V2. Requested by 67+ users.

steps.title: Check current traefik deployment
steps.description:
Verify your traefik version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
helm list -n traefik 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working traefik installation.

steps.title: Review traefik configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant traefik configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
kubectl get configmap -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=traefik
```
### Do you want to request a *feature* or report a *bug*?

Feature
### What did you do?

Using ACME to generate certificate for all of mine (sub)domains.
### What did you expect to see?

One certificate per domain.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Let's Encrypt: use ACME API V2
steps.description:
### What does this PR do?

This PR allows Træfik to use the new ACME version.
It uses the new [lego branch](https://github.com/xenolf/lego/tree/acmev2/acmev2) to access to the ACME V2 API.

ACME V2 API main features : 

- Suppression of `TLS-SNI-01` challenge
- Adding **wildcard certificates** generation with `DNS-02` challenge

### Motivation

Fix #2674 

Allowing users to do ACME wildcard certificates

### More

### Additional Notes

- For the moment, wildcard domains can only be defined

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Upgrade traefik to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade traefik:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade traefik traefik/traefik --namespace traefik
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n traefik
helm list -n traefik
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
kubectl get events -n traefik --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Let's Encrypt: use ACME API V2" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
### What does this PR do?

This PR allows Træfik to use the new ACME version.
It uses the new [lego branch](https://github.com/xenolf/lego/tree/acmev2/acmev2) to access to the ACME V2 API.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with traefik installed or the issue environment reproducible.
